content_of_post_to_comment: I don’t want to be “she’s fine, she can do everything alone, she doesn’t need help.” I want to be a dandelion woman. Existing softly, pretending I’m a helpless butterfly, saying, “I don’t know, I can’t, you do it.” Those women always get further. Meanwhile, if someone called me tomorrow and said, “We need you to go to war,” I’d be like, “Okay, just let me finish my coffee.”
comment: There’s an interesting gap between being capable and being cared for. Sometimes competence becomes a role people assign you, and the hardest part is making room for others to show up without having to perform weakness.
